message:
chore: Add gitignore rules for errant TypeScript build artifacts

Add patterns to ignore TypeScript compiled outputs that appear in source
directories due to IDE behavior or improper build invocations:

TypeScript artifacts in src/ directories:
- packages/*/src/**/*.js - compiled JavaScript (source is .ts)
- packages/*/src/**/*.d.ts - declaration files (should be in dist/)
- packages/*/src/**/*.js.map - source maps
- packages/*/src/**/*.d.ts.map - declaration maps

These files are errant because:
- tsconfig.json specifies outDir: "dist" for all packages
- Proper builds output to dist/, not src/
- These appear when IDE runs tsc incrementally or with wrong config

Temporary/scratch file patterns:
- reproduce*.ts - debugging reproduction files
- scratch*.ts - experimental scratch files
- temp*.ts - temporary files
- debug*.ts - debugging files

This prevents build artifacts from cluttering git status and accidentally
being committed alongside source files.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -9,6 +9,19 @@ out/
 .next/
 *.tsbuildinfo
 
+# TypeScript compiled outputs in source directories
+# (proper builds go to dist/, these are errant artifacts from IDE/improper builds)
+packages/*/src/**/*.js
+packages/*/src/**/*.d.ts
+packages/*/src/**/*.js.map
+packages/*/src/**/*.d.ts.map
+
+# Temporary/scratch files
+reproduce*.ts
+scratch*.ts
+temp*.ts
+debug*.ts
+
 # Generated database
 phage.db
